name: Passeig de Santa Madrona Chrono Point
roadSegment: Passeig de Santa Madrona / Point chrono n. 3 area on the Stage 1 Montjuic approach
estimatedPassageTime: 17:22-19:12 local from official time schedule area
summary: Technical Montjuic approach option near the official third chrono point before the Olympic Stadium finish.
whyItMatters: For a team time trial, this should give repeated, disciplined team formations late in the course, with riders already working uphill toward the final Montjuic sector.

access.estimated_route_from_base: Use Barcelona urban transit/walk approach to Montjuic; exact race-day transit changes are not published yet.
access.parking_transit_possibilities: Transit is preferable in principle. The Montjuic funicular reaches Parc de Montjuic from Paral-lel, but event-day service and course-crossing access must be verified.
access.walk_bike_approach_notes: Likely walk-in from Poble-sec/Paral-lel or from higher Montjuic paths; do not assume crossing the course after closures begin.
access.road_closure_notes: Unknown; no Barcelona 2026 race-day closure order found for this exact point.

pros: Official schedule identifies Passeig de Santa Madrona as a late-course chrono point.
Less finish-line-specific than the Olympic Stadium barrier, so it may have more flexible viewing angles.
Urban setting improves fallback options compared with isolated roadside points.
cons: Course-crossing and barrier layout may make the exact standing side unusable.
A city-circuit/time-trial area should be treated as high closure complexity until Barcelona publishes the traffic plan.
Food/toilet access nearby on maps does not prove reachability after barriers are installed.

logistics.parking: Unknown; do not plan to park inside Montjuic closure areas unless official parking is published.
logistics.transit: Montjuic funicular/metro approach is plausible but unverified for race day.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Needs official Barcelona municipal/Tour traffic order.

crowdRisk: medium-high: late-course Montjuic point on opening day, but probably less compressed than the finish line.
bathroomFoodProximity: Unknown on race day. Montjuic has museums/venues/cafes in normal conditions, but reachability after barriers is unverified.
escapeRoute: Walk downhill toward Poble-sec or Paral-lel after convoy clearance; exact exits depend on barriers and police directions.
safetyAssessment: Choose only a barriered or clearly off-road standing area; avoid corners, islands, and any position requiring spectators to lean into the race lane.

timing.best_arrival_window: Before the publicity caravan and before Montjuic access controls harden; exact closure time unknown.
timing.race_day_risk: High until local closures, pedestrian crossings, and transit changes are published.

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 Stage 1 official page
sources.note: Supports the Stage 1 date, Barcelona-Barcelona TTT, first start/last arrival, and time-schedule entries for Passeig de Santa Madrona and the Montjuic finish approach.

sources.title: TDF trip data official route note
sources.note: Official ASO ArcGIS layer referenced by the app for Stage 1 route geometry; coordinate is aligned with the app's Montjuic route points.

sources.title: Montjuic Funicular background
sources.note: Supports normal, non-event access concept between Paral-lel and Parc de Montjuic; does not prove race-day operation.

open_questions: Which side of Passeig de Santa Madrona remains publicly accessible after barriers are installed?
What are the official Barcelona road-closure and pedestrian-crossing times for Stage 1?
Will Montjuic funicular, buses, or nearby metro exits operate normally during the TTT?
